description: Expected simulation results for ocean-atmosphere biogeochemical coupling end-to-end test

coupling_verification.description: Verification that all coupling types function correctly in biogeochemical context

couple2_air_sea_exchange.flux_direction: CO2 typically ocean→atmosphere, O2 typically atmosphere→ocean

couple2_ocean_ecosystem.coupling_mechanism: Primary production consumes DIC, produces O2; respiration does reverse

variable_map.temporal_variation: Daily to seasonal cycles from CESM2 climate data

biogeochemical_operators: BiologicalPump: Export production to deep ocean

environmental_forcing: SeasonalLightCycle: Seasonal and diurnal solar radiation

ecosystem_events: ocean_acidification_threshold: Chemical response to low pH

conservation_principles: Global carbon conservation: atmospheric CO2 + oceanic DIC + ecosystem carbon = constant
Global oxygen conservation: atmospheric O2 + oceanic O2 = constant (+ biological sources/sinks)
Marine nitrogen conservation: nutrients + phytoplankton + zooplankton + detritus = constant
Alkalinity quasi-conservation: ALK changes only through CaCO3 formation/dissolution

O2_outgassing.biological_supersaturation: O2 production exceeds solubility during blooms

primary_production_effects.DIC_drawdown: 6.625 mol C per mol N fixed
primary_production_effects.O2_production: 1.4 mol O2 per mol N fixed

temperature_impacts.stratification: Warmer surface → deeper mixed layer in winter

wind_impacts.mixing_enhancement: Deeper mixed layers with stronger winds

expected_simulation_results.description: Expected numerical results for 1-year biogeochemical simulation

seasonal_cycles.description: Expected seasonal variations in key variables

atmospheric_CO2.driver: Northern hemisphere terrestrial carbon cycle proxy

ocean_pCO2.driver: Seasonal primary production and temperature cycles

latitudinal_gradients.nutrient_concentration: Low in tropics (5 μmol/kg), high in polar regions (25 μmol/kg)
latitudinal_gradients.primary_production: High in upwelling regions and high latitudes
latitudinal_gradients.ocean_CO2: Outgassing in tropics, uptake in high latitudes

seasonal_migration.bloom_progression: Equatorward in autumn, poleward in spring
seasonal_migration.nutrient_depletion: Follows primary production patterns with ~1 month lag

phytoplankton_blooms.trigger_frequency: 1-2 times per year in temperate zones

acidification_events.frequency: Rare in global mean, but possible in coastal upwelling
acidification_events.recovery_time: Weeks to months depending on CO2 flux magnitude

biogeochemical_fluxes.description: Expected magnitude and direction of biogeochemical fluxes

air_sea_O2_exchange.gas_exchange_rate: 2-3x faster than CO2 due to lower Schmidt number

validation_requirements.description: Specific validation requirements for biogeochemical coupling
mass_conservation_tests: Total carbon: atmospheric + oceanic + biological pools conserved to < 0.1%
Total oxygen: atmospheric + oceanic pools conserved to < 0.5%
Marine nitrogen: all ecosystem pools conserved to < 0.01%
Alkalinity: changes only through calcification (not implemented, so conserved)
flux_magnitude_tests: Air-sea CO2 flux: 0.1-50 mol C/m²/year range
Remineralization: matches export production integrated over year
seasonal_cycle_tests: Northern hemisphere CO2 minimum in August-September
Ocean pCO2 minimum during spring bloom periods
Primary production peaks in spring and autumn
Mixed layer depth maximum in winter months
event_response_tests: Bloom events trigger growth rate enhancement within 1 timestep
Nutrient upwelling events trigger ecosystem response within days
Event recovery follows exponential decay with appropriate timescales
coupling_consistency_tests: Connector equations maintain flux balance across system boundaries
Variable map updates propagate within same timestep
Callback updates occur at specified frequencies

performance_benchmarks.description: Expected computational performance for biogeochemical system

julia_mtk.solver_performance: CVODE_BDF optimal for stiff biogeochemical ODEs

concentration_values.note: Stricter tolerances for well-mixed atmospheric gases

flux_values.note: Biological fluxes more variable, allow larger tolerances

conservation_laws.note: Tightest conservation for marine nitrogen (closed system)
